company.name: Cadence Health
company.description: Cadence Health is a remote patient-monitoring platform for cardiology and chronic-care clinics. Connected devices stream vitals to our triage layer, which flags deterioration early and routes alerts to care teams. Strong clinical outcomes, but revenue is concentrated in one large health-system partner.

pitch_claims.unique_selling_point: Triage layer reduces false-alert fatigue by 44% in published clinic data, lifting clinician adoption.

customer_revenue_breakdown.note: Anchor contract through 2027; expansion to more sites under discussion but not yet signed.

unit_economics.note: Device-subsidy model compresses gross margin to 56%; margin expansion expected as device costs amortize.

red_flags: 58% of ARR from a single anchor health system
Gross margin of 56% — below software benchmark due to device subsidy

legal.ip_status: 1 patent pending on triage algorithm; clean invention-assignment agreements in place.

litigation.value: No active lawsuits or pending disputes.

regulatory_compliance.hipaa: HIPAA compliant; independent HIPAA controls audit completed 2026-01.
regulatory_compliance.device_status: Operates as non-device clinical decision-support software; outside FDA device scope under current product design.
regulatory_compliance.data_privacy: PHI encrypted; BAAs executed with all clinical customers.

security.last_penetration_test: Third-party penetration test completed 2026-02; findings remediated.
security.pii_storage: PHI encrypted at rest with AES-256; field-level encryption on identifiers.
security.mfa: MFA enforced for all clinical and admin users.

architecture.pattern: Event-driven ingestion from connected devices; redundant alert routing.
architecture.tech_debt_assessment: LOW-MODERATE — solid coverage; device-integration layer carries some bespoke code.

actual_market_trends.sector_growth: RPM reimbursement expansion drove 23% market growth in 2025.
actual_market_trends.policy_tailwinds: CMS reimbursement codes for remote monitoring continue to broaden coverage.
actual_market_trends.demand: Health systems are expanding chronic-care-at-home programs.

market.positioning: Cadence differentiates on triage quality and clinician adoption rather than raw device connectivity.

ceo.name: Dr. Aisha Rahman
ceo.previous_company: Practicing cardiologist; clinical informatics lead at an academic medical center
ceo.assessment: Strong clinical credibility and buyer empathy; first-time operator scaling go-to-market.

cto.name: Tom Becker
cto.previous: Senior engineer at a connected-health startup (acquired)
cto.assessment: Solid; has shipped regulated health software before.

team_gaps: No CFO yet — finance handled by a fractional controller; a Series A close should fund a full-time hire.
Revenue concentration means a dedicated enterprise-expansion lead is needed.

deal_summary.existing_investor_participation: Bessemer indicated pro-rata participation pending lead.
deal_summary.note: Strong clinical product and clean legal/technical posture; concerns are financial — runway, margin, and single-partner concentration. A conditional term sheet with milestone-based tranches is the likely path.
